data.body:
MANUAL PLAN-REVIEW (5fd2 three-pass) of the UPDATED epic 5fd2 (messy-moose-jig) after adding the Pass-4 coach.

PROCESS: 5fd2 is an epic WITH children and session-log-linked (63cc) → applicable set = epic-level + container
(G3/G4) + ISF (21 criteria). 3 independent finders (plan-text incl. coach-coherence / agent-tier grounded /
container+ISF over the 14-child manifest) → 1 independent verifier (charitable, complete-artifact bar, repo+ticket
grounded) → deterministic Pass-3. DET floor P1-P8: pass/fail-open. 0 BLOCKS.

RESULT: 5 findings → 1 ADVISORY + 4 DROPPED. The gate caught a real hole in the coach design we just built.

ADVISORY (conf 0.86) [T8/G6] — `subject` free-text can smuggle a solution → C1 NOT enforced "by construction".
The coach's `subject` is an LLM-extracted free-text field the locked template interpolates verbatim, but it has
no validator/length-bound/noun-phrase constraint, so a model can satisfy {move_id, subject, finding_refs} while
putting a solution in `subject` (e.g. move_id=5, subject="use a mutex around the check-then-act to fix the
TOCTOU"). The "deterministic render" only locks the PROSE the LLM would author, not the one field it DOES author.
FIX: constrain/validate `subject` (short noun-phrase only; length bound; reject code/imperative) so C1 holds
mechanically — add to child 75a9 (the project already enforces field bounds in src/rebar/llm/structured.py / findings.py).

DROPPED but TRUE (evidence_entails=yes; gate filtered as low-impact/mitigatable — both cheap, worth fixing):
- conf 0.43 [COH/E1] the dedicated "Review structure — three-pass" section still lists only Pass 1/2/3; Pass-4 is
  only in the design summary. Charitably the section is scoped to the verdict/decision model (coach is post-
  decision), so dropped — but a one-line mention of Pass-4 in that section removes the ambiguity. (My edit.)
- conf 0.43 [G4/G3] the finding-`id` contract is asserted by 75a9 + db7b but minted by NO child: the Pass-1
  finder 1913 emit contract {finding,criteria,evidence,scenarios,impact} has no `id` and wasn't updated; 75a9↔db7b
  reference each other circularly. Dropped (mitigatable — the orchestrator ca03 could mint at assembly), but a real
  coordination gap. FIX: assign id-minting to ONE owner (1913 or ca03); make 75a9/db7b consumers. NB: this is
  exactly what coaching move #11 (propagate revisions to children) / #12 (does this apply elsewhere) exist to catch
  — I propagated the id contract to the consumers but missed the producer. The coach would have coached me here.

DROPPED & refuted (evidence_entails=no — charitable reading satisfies):
- conf 0.29 [E1] eval coverage — the coach's move-mapping eval IS specified, in 75a9's own AC ("Tested: finding→
  move mapping on a labelled set"), just not in the standing-suite AC (reasonable scoping).
- conf 0.29 [T8] move-selection dual-spec — resolvable: the LLM emits move_id (selection); `triggers` reads as the
  registry's declarative hint (criteria-registry sibling pattern); "no move → no coaching" = an empty cluster (the
  finding simply absent from finding_refs), no sentinel needed.

ISF: clean (the coach is additive, never alters the Pass-3 decision, descopes nothing in the recorded plan-of-record).
G3: the new Pass-4 AC is covered by child 75a9. Decomposition/ordering (75a9 depends_on Pass-3 487d) coherent.

META: dogfood win — the gate, run on the coach we just designed, surfaced 1 genuine design hole (C1/subject not
enforced by construction) and correctly filtered the rest; and the dropped-but-true finding-id gap is precisely the
incomplete-propagation that coaching move #11 is designed to prevent.
